title: Limonium pomelianum
intro: Limonium pomegraniatum, also known as Sea Lavender or Statice, is a perennial succulent plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its vibrant purple flowers and silvery-gray foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing inflammation, and protecting against infections. Bioactive constituents of Limonium pomegraniatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as dried flowers for use in potpourri and crafts.
taxonomy: Limonium pomelianum is a species of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. The genus Limonium is a group of succulent plants commonly known as sea lavender or statice. This genus belongs to the order Plumbaginales, which includes other families like Plumbaginaceae and Limoniaceae. Limonium pomelianum, as a species, is a member of the class Magnoliopsida, which includes flowering plants with a two-lobed cotyledon. It is part of the kingdom Plantae, which encompasses all plant life.
